text: Supervised fine-{{c1::tuning}} trains a model on task-specific examples to improve behavior in a target domain.
explanation: SFT is often the first step when you want a general model to become useful for a business workflow.
usage: Common in domain assistants, classification, and structured extraction tasks.
spanish: El fine-tuning supervisado entrena un modelo con ejemplos específicos de tarea para mejorar su comportamiento en un dominio objetivo.

text: LoRA and {{c1::QLoRA}} adapt models by updating a small number of low-rank parameters rather than all weights.
explanation: These methods make fine-tuning cheaper, faster, and more accessible for smaller teams.
usage: Widely used for private, domain-specific, and cost-effective model adaptation.
spanish: LoRA y QLoRA adaptan modelos actualizando un pequeño número de parámetros de bajo rango en lugar de todos los pesos.

text: {{c1::Direct Preference Optimization}} trains on preference pairs so the model more strongly prefers better responses.
explanation: DPO is often simpler and more stable than PPO for preference alignment.
usage: Useful when optimizing helpfulness, tone, and safety on chat models.
spanish: Direct Preference Optimization entrena con pares de preferencias para que el modelo prefiera respuestas mejores.

text: {{c1::Proximal Policy Optimization}} is a reinforcement learning method used to align models with reward signals.
explanation: PPO is powerful but less lightweight than SFT or DPO, often requiring more engineering effort.
usage: Used in RLHF pipelines and reward-driven agent behavior.
spanish: Proximal Policy Optimization es un método de aprendizaje por refuerzo usado para alinear modelos con señales de recompensa.

text: {{c1::Synthetic data}} generation can create large training sets when real labels are scarce or expensive.
explanation: It is powerful but must be monitored carefully to avoid introducing bias or feedback loops.
usage: Common in classification, generation, and multilingual tasks.
spanish: La generación de datos sintéticos puede crear grandes conjuntos de entrenamiento cuando las etiquetas reales son escasas o costosas.

text: {{c1::Domain adaptation}} tailors a model to new contexts such as finance, medicine, or engineering without fully retraining it.
explanation: This is often more practical than training from scratch for specialized tasks.
usage: Critical in regulated and industry-specific AI products.
spanish: La adaptación a dominio ajusta un modelo a nuevos contextos como finanzas, medicina o ingeniería sin entrenarlo de cero.

text: Catastrophic forgetting happens when fine-{{c1::tuning}} erases previously learned capabilities.
explanation: A good adaptation strategy preserves core abilities while improving the target behavior.
usage: Important for multi-task assistants and long-lived model variants.
spanish: El olvido catastrófico ocurre cuando el fine-tuning borra capacidades previamente aprendidas.

text: The quality of the training data often matters more than the algorithm choice when {{c1::fine}}-tuning a model.
explanation: Noisy, inconsistent, or biased data produces brittle behavior in the adapted model.
usage: Central in reliable enterprise AI pipelines.
spanish: La calidad de los datos de entrenamiento a menudo importa más que la elección del algoritmo al fine-tunar un modelo.

scenario: Math & Physics 📐 (Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) Weight Update (Hu et al., 2021))
text: Ecuación para {{c1::Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) Weight Update (Hu et al., 2021)}}: \[W = W_0 + \Delta W = W_0 + \frac{\alpha}{r} B \cdot A\]
explanation:
Desglose de variables:
  • \(W_0\): Frozen pre-trained weight matrix of shape \((d, k)\).
  • \(\Delta W\): Low-rank weight update matrix.
  • \(A\): Down-projection matrix of shape \((r, k)\) initialized with a Gaussian distribution.
  • \(B\): Up-projection matrix of shape \((d, r)\) initialized to zero.
  • \(r\): The bottleneck rank parameter (\(r \ll \min(d, k)\)).
  • \(\alpha\): Constant scaling factor.
usage: Formula: \[W = W_0 + \Delta W = W_0 + \frac{\alpha}{r} B \cdot A\]
spanish: Fórmula de Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) Weight Update (Hu et al., 2021)

scenario: Code Pattern 💻 (PyTorch LoRA Linear Layer Implementation)
text:
Patrón de código en python para {{c1::PyTorch LoRA Linear Layer Implementation}}:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import math

class LoRALinear(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, r=8, alpha=16):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.linear.weight.requires_grad = False  # Freeze W0
        self.lora_A = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(r, in_features))
        self.lora_B = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(out_features, r))
        self.scaling = alpha / r
        nn.init.kaiming_uniform_(self.lora_A, a=math.sqrt(5))

    def forward(self, x):
        base_out = self.linear(x)
        lora_out = (x @ self.lora_A.t() @ self.lora_B.t()) * self.scaling
        return base_out + lora_out
explanation: Defines a custom PyTorch layer where the pre-trained linear weight \(W_0\) is frozen, and a parallel trainable low-rank pathway (matrices A and B) is added and scaled by \(\alpha / r\).
usage:
Snippet: 
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import math

class LoRALinear(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, r=8, alpha=16):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.linear.weight.requires_grad = False  # Freeze W0
        self.lora_A = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(r, in_features))
        self.lora_B = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(out_features, r))
        self.scaling = alpha / r
        nn.init.kaiming_uniform_(self.lora_A, a=math.sqrt(5))

    def forward(self, x):
        base_out = self.linear(x)
        lora_out = (x @ self.lora_A.t() @ self.lora_B.t()) * self.scaling
        return base_out + lora_out

Output expected: Success
spanish: Patrón de código: PyTorch LoRA Linear Layer Implementation

text:
Pregunta: Which three key optimizations are introduced in QLoRA (Dettmers et al., 2023) to enable finetuning 65B models on a single 48GB GPU?
Opciones:
  • A) 4-bit NormalFloat (NF4) representation, Double Quantization, and Paged Optimizers
  • B) 8-bit Integer Quantization, Gradient Checkpointing, and Low-Rank Adaptation
  • C) FP8 format weights, CPU offloading, and Zero Redundancy Optimizer (ZeRO)
  • D) Tensor Parallelism, pipeline parallel execution, and activation caching

Respuesta correcta: {{c1::A) 4-bit NormalFloat (NF4) representation, Double Quantization, and Paged Optimizers}}
explanation: Justificación: QLoRA stores the base model in 4-bit NormalFloat (NF4) format (information-theoretically optimal for normally distributed weights), applies Double Quantization (quantizing the quantization constants, saving 0.37 bits/parameter), and uses Paged Optimizers (utilizing CUDA Unified Memory to prevent out-of-memory spikes during gradient updates).
usage: Quiz assertion: A) 4-bit NormalFloat (NF4) representation, Double Quantization, and Paged Optimizers
spanish: Quiz: Which three key optimizations are introduced in QLoRA (Dettmers et al., 2023) to enable finetuning 65B models on a single 48GB GPU?
